backend._comment_gpu_layers: n_gpu_layers=-1 offloads all transformer layers to GPU. Set to 0 for CPU-only, or a positive integer for partial offload (e.g. 20 for ~60% of a 7B model). Each layer costs ~120-200MB VRAM depending on model architecture and quantization.
backend._comment_ctx: n_ctx=4096 is the safe default for single-GPU 8GB (RTX 2000 Ada). For 16GB GPU (RTX 5060 Ti), can go up to 8192-16384 with 7B Q4_K_M. Context VRAM cost: ~2MB per 1K tokens for 7B models. 128K context on 7B requires ~25GB — will OOM on consumer cards.
backend._comment_flash_attn: flash_attention=true reduces KV cache VRAM by ~50% and improves throughput at long contexts. Requires SM>=80 (Ampere+). Both RTX 2000 Ada (SM89) and RTX 5060 Ti (SM120) support this.

roofline_model.description: Expected tok/s by model size and quantization on this hardware

context_vram_cost.description: Approximate additional VRAM per 1K context tokens (KV cache)
context_vram_cost.7B_model: ~2 MB/1K tokens (with flash attention), ~4 MB/1K tokens (without)

optimization_checklist: Set n_gpu_layers=-1 for full GPU offload (verify with nvidia-smi that model fits)
Enable flash_attention=true on SM>=80 GPUs to halve KV cache VRAM
Keep n_ctx as small as your workload allows — VRAM scales linearly with context
Use Q4_K_M quantization as the default quality/speed tradeoff
For dual-GPU: set OLLAMA_SCHED_SPREAD=true to distribute layers
For single-user: set OLLAMA_NUM_PARALLEL=1 and OLLAMA_MAX_LOADED_MODELS=1
Run Test-PcaiGpuReadiness -ModelPath <gguf> to verify model fits before loading
